panels.title: Cluster CPU Usage (millicores, all pods summed)
panels.description: Total CPU used across every pod, per scrape (60s). Node has 4000 mcores — distance from that line is headroom. Useful for seeing aggregate demand trends and burst peaks.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "kube_metrics")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "k8s_pod_resources" and r._field == "cpu_millicores")
  |> group(columns: ["_time"])
  |> sum(column: "_value")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> yield(name: "cluster_cpu")

panels.title: Cluster Memory Usage (MiB, all pods summed)
panels.description: Total memory used across every pod, per scrape. Node has ~4880 MiB allocatable — headroom = that minus this line.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "kube_metrics")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "k8s_pod_resources" and r._field == "memory_kb")
  |> group(columns: ["_time"])
  |> sum(column: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value / 1024.0 }))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> yield(name: "cluster_mem")

panels.title: Per-pod CPU over time (mcores)
panels.description: One line per pod. Toggle pods in the legend to isolate workloads. Look for tall/narrow spikes = bursty pods; flat high lines = steady consumers.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "kube_metrics")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "k8s_pod_resources" and r._field == "cpu_millicores" and (r.namespace == "${namespace}" or "${namespace}" == "all"))
  |> group(columns: ["namespace", "pod"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "pod"])
  |> yield(name: "per_pod_cpu")

panels.title: Per-pod Memory over time (MiB)
panels.description: One line per pod. Memory is usually steady — rising lines may indicate a leak. Spikes = transient allocations (embedding loads, GC).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "kube_metrics")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "k8s_pod_resources" and r._field == "memory_kb" and (r.namespace == "${namespace}" or "${namespace}" == "all"))
  |> group(columns: ["namespace", "pod"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value / 1024.0 }))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "pod"])
  |> yield(name: "per_pod_mem")

panels.title: Peak & Avg per pod (selected time range)
panels.description: THE sizing table. For each pod: peak and average CPU (mcores) and memory (MiB) over the selected range. Use Peak to set limits, Avg+Peak to set requests. Sort by any column. Pick the time range (top-right) to size against a representative window.

targets.query:
cpu = from(bucket: "kube_metrics")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "k8s_pod_resources" and r._field == "cpu_millicores" and (r.namespace == "${namespace}" or "${namespace}" == "all"))
  |> group(columns: ["namespace", "pod"])

cpu_max = cpu |> max(column: "_value") |> set(key: "_field", value: "cpu_peak_mcores")
cpu_avg = cpu |> mean(column: "_value") |> set(key: "_field", value: "cpu_avg_mcores")

mem = from(bucket: "kube_metrics")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "k8s_pod_resources" and r._field == "memory_kb" and (r.namespace == "${namespace}" or "${namespace}" == "all"))
  |> group(columns: ["namespace", "pod"])

mem_max = mem |> max(column: "_value") |> set(key: "_field", value: "mem_peak_mib") |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value / 1024.0 }))
mem_avg = mem |> mean(column: "_value") |> set(key: "_field", value: "mem_avg_mib") |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value / 1024.0 }))

union(tables: [cpu_max, cpu_avg, mem_max, mem_avg])
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["namespace", "pod"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> keep(columns: ["namespace", "pod", "cpu_peak_mcores", "cpu_avg_mcores", "mem_peak_mib", "mem_avg_mib"])
  |> sort(columns: ["cpu_peak_mcores"], desc: true)
  |> yield(name: "pod_summary")

title: CE AI Lab — Pod Resource Utilization
